title: Big Ben and Westminster Palace
titleZh: 大本钟与威斯敏斯特宫

q.en: According to the book, what was the name 'Big Ben' first actually used to name?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，'大本钟'(Big Ben)这个名字最初实际上是用在哪样东西上的？

options.en: The enormous bronze bell behind the clock faces at the top of the tower.
options.zh: 塔顶钟面后方那口巨大的青铜大钟。

options.en: The river that flows past Westminster Palace.
options.zh: 流经威斯敏斯特宫的那条河。

options.en: The king who ordered the tower to be built.
options.zh: 下令建造这座塔的国王。

options.en: The clock tower itself, now called the Elizabeth Tower. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 钟楼本身，也就是现在的伊丽莎白塔。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says the name Big Ben is often used for the clock tower or the clock, but it was first used to name the enormous bell behind the clock faces at the top of the tower.
explain.zh: 书中说人们常把'大本钟'用来指钟楼或大钟，但它最初是用来命名塔顶钟面后方那口巨大铜钟的。

q.en: The book gives two stories for how Big Ben got its name. Besides Sir Benjamin Hall, the other popular story centers on which person?
q.zh: 书中给出了大本钟得名的两种说法。除了本杰明·霍尔爵士之外，另一种流行说法围绕着哪个人物？

options.en: Charles Barry, the architect of the palace.
options.zh: 查尔斯·巴里，宫殿的建筑师。

options.en: Benjamin Caunt, a heavyweight boxing champion of the time who was also nicknamed Big Ben.
options.zh: 本杰明·考恩特，当时的重量级拳击冠军，同样绰号'大本'。

options.en: Queen Elizabeth II, who renamed the tower in 2012. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 伊丽莎白二世女王，她在2012年重新命名了这座塔。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: King Henry VIII.
options.zh: 亨利八世国王。

explain.en: The book says one story centers on Sir Benjamin Hall, and the other popular story centers on Benjamin Caunt, a big heavyweight boxing champion nicknamed Big Ben.
explain.zh: 书中说一种说法围绕本杰明·霍尔爵士，另一种流行说法围绕本杰明·考恩特——一位体型高大、绰号'大本'的重量级拳击冠军。

q.en: When was the great bell of Big Ben cast?
q.zh: 大本钟的巨钟是在何时铸造的？

options.en: Between 1097 and 1099.
In 1889, for a World's Fair.
options.zh: 1889年，为一场世界博览会。

options.en: On November 5, 1605.

explain.en: The book states the bell was cast in East London on April 10, 1858, and that this began Big Ben's journey through history.
explain.zh: 书中说这口钟于1858年4月10日在伦敦东区铸造，从此开启了大本钟的历史旅程。

q.en: The huge wooden roof of Westminster Hall that exists today was added in 1393 by which king?
q.zh: 今天威斯敏斯特厅那巨大的木制屋顶是由哪位国王在1393年增建的？

options.en: King Henry VIII.
options.zh: 亨利八世国王。

options.en: King James I.
options.zh: 詹姆斯一世国王。

options.en: King William II, who built the original hall.
options.zh: 威廉二世国王，他建造了最初的厅堂。

options.en: King Richard II, in 1393.
options.zh: 理查二世国王，于1393年。

explain.en: The book says Westminster Hall was built under William II but the huge wooden ceiling that exists today was added in 1393 by King Richard II.
explain.zh: 书中说威斯敏斯特厅是在威廉二世时期建造的，但今天存在的巨大木制天花板的木顶是在1393年由理查二世增建的。

q.en: The book quotes the folk verse 'Remember, remember! / The fifth of November...' Why does the author include this poem?
q.zh: 书中引用了民谣诗句'记住，记住！/ 十一月五日……'。作者为何加入这首诗？

options.en: To explain how the clock mechanism works. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 解释钟楼机械装置如何运作。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To list the architects of the palace.
options.zh: 列出宫殿的建筑师。

options.en: To describe the height of the tower.
options.zh: 描述高塔的高度。

options.en: To bring the 1605 Gunpowder Plot to life and show how it is remembered in British tradition.
options.zh: 让1605年的火药阴谋鲜活起来，并展现它如何被英国传统所铭记。

explain.en: The author uses the period verse about the Fifth of November to evoke the Gunpowder Plot of 1605 and show how the event lives on in British culture and celebration.
explain.zh: 作者借这首关于'十一月五日'的当代民谣，唤起1605年火药阴谋的史实，并展现这一事件如何留存于英国文化与庆祝传统之中。

q.en: The book is organized into chapters such as 'Who or What Is Big Ben?', 'The History of Westminster', 'The Fifth of November', 'Fire and Reconstruction', and 'The Towers of Westminster'. What does this organization reflect?
q.zh: 本书的章节包括'大本钟是什么/谁'、'威斯敏斯特的历史'、'十一月五日'、'火灾与重建'、'威斯敏斯特的塔楼'等。这种组织结构反映了什么？

options.en: Only the step-by-step construction of the tower. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 仅仅按步骤讲述高塔的建造过程。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: A logical progression from what Big Ben is, to its history, key events, and its lasting status as a symbol.
options.zh: 一种从'大本钟是什么'到其历史、关键事件，再到其作为象征的持久地位的逻辑推进。

options.en: A random collection of unrelated facts.
options.zh: 一堆互不相关事实的随机罗列。

options.en: Strictly reverse chronological order.
options.zh: 严格的倒叙时间顺序。

explain.en: The chapter order moves from defining Big Ben, through its history and dramatic events like the Gunpowder Plot and the 1834 fire, to its towers and icon status, showing a clear historical-developmental structure.
explain.zh: 章节顺序从界定大本钟，到其历史与火药阴谋、1834年大火等戏剧性事件，再到塔楼与地标地位，呈现出清晰的历史—发展性结构。

q.en: How did Big Ben and the Palace of Westminster fare during the intense German bombing of World War II?
q.zh: 在第二次世界大战猛烈的德国轰炸中，大本钟与威斯敏斯特宫情况如何？

options.en: The clock stopped forever in 1940.
options.zh: 大钟在1940年永远停摆。

options.en: The entire palace was completely demolished.
options.zh: 整座宫殿被彻底拆毁。

options.en: Big Ben survived and continued to chime every fifteen minutes throughout the bombing.
options.zh: 大本钟幸存下来，并在整个轰炸期间持续每十五分钟鸣响一次。

options.en: Big Ben was destroyed and only rebuilt after the war. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 大本钟被摧毁，直到战后才重建。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says that throughout intense German bombing during WWII, Big Ben survived and continued to chime every fifteen minutes, even though nine months of 1940 bombing destroyed many historic London buildings.
explain.zh: 书中说在整个二战期间的猛烈轰炸中，大本钟幸存下来并持续每十五分钟鸣响一次，尽管1940年长达九个月的轰炸摧毁了伦敦许多历史建筑。

q.en: Westminster Palace has three main towers. According to the book, which one is the tallest?
q.zh: 威斯敏斯特宫有三座主要塔楼。根据书中内容，哪一座最高？

options.en: The Houses of Parliament, at 100 meters.
options.zh: 议会两院，高100米。

options.en: The Victoria Tower, at 98.5 meters (323 ft.).
options.zh: 维多利亚塔，高98.5米（323英尺）。

options.en: The Central Tower, at 91.4 meters (300 ft.).
The Elizabeth Tower, at 96.31 meters (316 ft.).
options.zh: 伊丽莎白塔，高96.31米（316英尺）。

explain.en: The book states the Victoria Tower is the tallest, standing at 98.5 meters (323 ft.), completed in 1860; the Elizabeth Tower is 96.31 m and the Central Tower is 91.4 m.
explain.zh: 书中说维多利亚塔最高，达98.5米（323英尺），于1860年竣工；伊丽莎白塔为96.31米，中央塔为91.4米。

q.en: The book's Focus Question asks what makes Big Ben and Westminster Palace significant to England's history. What is the author's main purpose in writing the book?
q.zh: 本书的聚焦问题是：是什么让大本钟与威斯敏斯特宫对英格兰历史具有重要意义？作者写作本书的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To explain the historical and cultural significance of Big Ben and Westminster Palace.
options.zh: 解释大本钟与威斯敏斯特宫在历史上和文化上的重要意义。

options.en: To tell a fictional story about a king.
options.zh: 讲述一个关于国王的虚构故事。

options.en: To argue that the palace should be demolished. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 主张这座宫殿应当被拆除。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To teach readers how to build a clock.
options.zh: 教读者如何建造一座钟。

explain.en: The Focus Question 'What makes Big Ben and Westminster Palace significant to England's history?' shows the author's purpose is to explain the site's historical and cultural importance.
explain.zh: 聚焦问题'是什么让大本钟与威斯敏斯特宫对英格兰历史具有重要意义？'表明作者的目的是解释这一地点在历史上与文化上的重要性。

q.en: The book describes curious rules inside Parliament, such as that no monarch may enter the House of Commons (the last was Charles I in 1642) and that, by a seven-hundred-year-old belief, no one may die within Westminster Palace. Why include these details?
q.zh: 书中描述了议会内部一些奇特的规矩，例如任何君主都不得进入下议院（最后一位进入的是1642年的查理一世），并且根据一条七百年的老传统，任何人不得在威斯敏斯特宫内去世。作者为何加入这些细节？

options.en: To list the members of Parliament.
options.zh: 列出议会议员。

options.en: To describe why the tower tilts.
options.zh: 说明高塔为何倾斜。

options.en: To highlight the unusual living traditions that give the palace its distinctive character.
options.zh: 凸显那些赋予宫殿独特气质的、非同寻常的活传统。

options.en: To explain exactly how laws are passed. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 确切解释法律是如何通过的。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: By sharing these odd but real customs, the author adds human interest and reveals the long-lived traditions that make the institution distinctive.
explain.zh: 通过分享这些古怪却真实的习俗，作者增添了人情趣味，也揭示了让这一机构独具特色、源远流长的传统。

prompt.en: The book notes the Elizabeth Tower tilts slightly because of nearby digging, yet it is not in danger of falling. If you could visit, would you rather climb the 334 steps to see Big Ben up close, or watch the New Year's Eve fireworks along the Thames? Explain using details from the book.
prompt.zh: 书中提到伊丽莎白塔因附近施工而略微倾斜，但并无倒塌危险。如果能去参观，你更愿意攀登334级台阶近距离看大本钟，还是到泰晤士河畔看新年前夜的烟花？请用书中细节说明你的选择。

en: Mention the 334-step climb and the behind-the-scenes view of the clocks described in the book.
You could also mention the New Year's Eve fireworks gathering along the Thames mentioned at the end.
zh: 提及书中描述的334级台阶攀登，以及近距离观看大钟内部机械的幕后体验。
你也可以提到书末所说的泰晤士河畔新年前夜烟花集会。
